key_insight: SimPhysNet combines physics-informed self-supervision with few-shot prototypical classification to predict laser welding penetration accurately from very limited labeled images.
engineering_relevance: This reduces labeling burden while supporting reliable real-time weld quality monitoring and automation in laser welding production.
method: Use a PINN inside a contrastive self-supervised framework to learn physically meaningful molten-pool/keyhole features from unlabeled images.
Apply three image augmentation tasks to improve feature robustness and generalization.
Classify penetration state with a few-shot prototypical network built from a small labeled set.
evidence_signal: The model reached 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset, and was reported as comparable to fully supervised methods.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the physics priors, unlabeled pool/keyhole imagery, and the specific welding setup.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
